title: Miscellaneous Instructional Policies: Teaching About Controversial Issues
description: Teachers and staff follow clear guidelines when discussing controversial or sensitive topics in the classroom, ensuring that instruction remains balanced and age-appropriate. The policy helps protect students' rights to form their own opinions while making sure no single political or ideological viewpoint is promoted over others. Parents and community members can trust that classroom discussions on complex issues are handled thoughtfully and in line with district standards.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's EMB (Local) policy on Teaching About Controversial Issues significantly expands the policy by replacing "teachers" with the broader term "Subject Persons" (defined to include employees, volunteers, and contractors), and adds numerous new sections aligned with S.B. 12 (89th Texas Legislature) covering holidays/commemorative months, human sexuality instruction with parental consent requirements, pornographic material prohibition, diversity/equity/inclusion restrictions, gender identity and sexual orientation prohibitions, social transitioning, pronoun usage, prohibited terms, and new discipline and superintendent authority provisions.
changes_added: Subject Person Defined section heading added (as new sidebar label)

As used in this policy, "Subject Persons" means those persons who (1) are employed by the District, (2) volunteer time or services on behalf of the District (3) directly interact with District students including contractors, vendors, and other individuals. [moved to top of policy as its own defined section]
Subject Persons [replacing "Teachers"] shall not use the classroom to transmit personal beliefs regarding political or sectarian issues.
Subject Persons and sStudents [replacing "and educators"] shall ensure that, to the extent possible, discussions are conducted fairly and courteously.

Unless required by state or federal law or the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills, Subject Persons [replacing "Teachers"] shall not be compelled to discuss widely debated and currently controversial issues
or it is required by state or federal law or the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills, [added condition for when discussion may be required]
Subject Person [replacing "teacher"] must explore that topic objectively and in a manner free from political bias.

In guiding [replacing "classroom"] discussion of controversial issues, Subject Persons [replacing "teachers"] shall comply with the instructional requirements and prohibitions imposed under Texas law and by the District.
Subject Person [replacing "teacher"] shall be certain that:
Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills for that grade level, [added to item 1 under selection criteria]
If a Subject Person [replacing "teacher"] is unsure about a topic of discussion or about the methods to employ, the Subject Person [replacing "teacher"] may discuss the issue with the principal.
In guiding [replacing "classroom"] discussion of controversial issues, Subject Persons [replacing "teachers"] shall: [Classroom Discussion section]
Holidays or Commemorative Months [new section heading]

In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), the District may acknowledge or teach the significance of state and federal holidays or commemorative months only if: 1. The federal or state holiday or commemorative month is established through a Presidential Proclamation, Congressional law or Resolution, Gubernatorial Proclamation, or Texas legislative law or Resolution; and 2. Fits into the themes of history and the stories of this state and the United States of America in accordance with the TEKS under Subchapter A, Chapter 28.
Except as required by state or federal law, tThe District, including Subject Persons [replacing "its teachers, and administrators, or any third party,"] shall not:
provide guidance or activities, discuss, require an understanding, [added to item 1 prohibition list]
discomfort, anguish, or guilt, or any other form of psychological distress [expanded language in item 1.e]
i. Make unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments. [new item i added to prohibition list]

Critical Race Theory and Systemic Discrimination Ideologies are generally defined as academic frameworks that laws, policies, and institutions create and maintain racial inequality, even when they appear neutral. [added definition of CRT/SDI]

Except as required by state or federal law, no private funding shall be accepted by the District for the purposes of curriculum development, purchase or choice of curricular materials, teacher training, or professional development pertaining to the above topics or Board Policy CH (Local), EFA (Local) or EFB (Local).
Student Lobbying for Course Credit [new section heading]

In accordance with HB 3979 (87th Texas Legislature), no employee shall require, make part of a course, or award course grading or credit including extra credit for, student work for, affiliation with, or service learning in association with any organization engaged in lobbying for legislation at the local, state or federal level, or in social or public policy advocacy. No employee shall require, make part of a course, or award course grading or credit including extra credit for, political activism, lobbying, or efforts to persuade members of the legislative or executive branch to take specific actions by direct communication at the local, state or federal level, or any practicum or like activity involving social or public policy advocacy.
Instruction on Human Sexuality [new section heading]

No human sexuality instruction may be provided that is inconsistent with the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills. In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), before a student may be provided with human sexuality instruction by a Subject Person, the District must obtain the written consent of the student's parent. A request for written consent under this subsection: 1. May not be included with any other notification or request for written consent provided to the parent, other than the notice, and 2. Must be provided to the parent not later than the 30th day before the date on which the human sexuality instruction begins.

The consent must include: 1. A clear, plain-language summary of the human sexuality curriculum (topics covered, objectives, grade-appropriateness). 2. Identification of whether outside speakers or third-party curriculum providers will be used. 3. Dates and class periods when the instruction will occur. 4. A statement that participation requires parental consent under S.B. 12. 5. Confirmation that the notice is provided at least 30 days prior to instruction (to comply with subsection (2)). 6. An explanation of the right to review the instructional materials before consenting. 7. A statement of the parent's right to withhold consent without penalty to the student. 8. A reminder that parents may revoke consent at any time by written notice. 9. Clear checkboxes or signature lines for: "I give my consent for my child to participate in human sexuality instruction," or "I do not give my consent for my child to participate," and Space for parent/guardian signature and date. 10. Name and contact information for the administrator or office handling the curriculum (so parents can ask questions or review materials).
Pornographic Material Prohibition [new section heading]

Pornographic material includes, but not limited to, "Harmful material" as defined by Section 43.24, Penal Code; "indecent content" that portrays sexual or excretory organs or activities in a way that is patently offensive; "profane content" including grossly offensive language that is considered a public nuisance; a library material rated sexually explicit material by the selling library material vendor; "sexually explicit material" as defined by Education Code 33.021; library material containing indecent content or profane content; any material that is pervasively vulgar or educationally unsuitable as referenced in Board of Education v. Pico; any material that refers a person to an Internet website containing pornographic material as described in this section of this Policy, including by use of a link or QR code, as defined by Section 443.001 of the Texas Health and Safety Code, or any other material legally prohibited from inclusion in a public school library. [See EFB(LEGAL) and EMB(Local)]. The possession, acquisition, and purchase of pornographic library materials is prohibited.

Unless required by state or federal law or the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills, instructional materials and library materials shall not include pornographic materials. Subject persons shall not instruct, or train any Subject Person to teach, support, or promote Pornographic Material.
Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion [new section heading]
Definitions [new sub-heading under DEI]

In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), diversity, equity, and inclusion duties means: 1. Influencing hiring or employment practices with respect to race, sex, color, or ethnicity except as necessary to comply with state or federal antidiscrimination laws; 2. Promoting differential treatment of or providing special benefits to individuals on the basis of race, color, or ethnicity; 3. Required any District employee or student to engage in training, orientation, or therapy that presents any form of race or sex stereotyping or blame on the basis of race or sex. (HB 3979, 87th Texas Legislature). 4. Developing or implementing policies, procedures, trainings, activities, or programs that reference race, color, ethnicity, gender identity, or sexual orientation except: (A) for the purpose of student recruitment efforts by colleges and universities designated as historically black colleges and universities in collaboration with school districts or open-enrollment charter schools; or (B) as necessary to comply with state or federal law. 5. Developing or producing Equity Audits based on race, color, ethnicity, gender identity, or sexual orientation, except the District may analyze school-based cause and take steps to eliminate unlawful discriminatory practices as necessary to address achievement gaps and differentials described by Texas Education Code Section 39.053. 6. Establishing offices or employment positions or assignments related to diversity, equity, and inclusion duties.
Inclusion Statement [new sub-heading under DEI]

In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), the District may not compel, require, induce, or solicit any person to provide a diversity, equity, and inclusion statement or giving preferential consideration to any person based on the provision of a diversity, equity, and inclusion statement.
Prohibition [new sub-heading under DEI]

In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), except as required by state or federal law, a school district: (1) may not assign diversity, equity, and inclusion duties to any person; and (2) shall prohibit a district employee, contractor, or volunteer from engaging in diversity, equity, and inclusion duties at, for, or on behalf of the district.
Contracting Clarification [new sub-heading under DEI]

Nothing in this Policy may be construed to limit or prohibit the District from contracting with historically underutilized businesses or businesses owned by members of a minority group or by women in accordance with applicable state law.
Gender Identity [replacing "Fluidity"] and Sexual Orientation [section heading updated]

Sexual orientation means sexual attraction a person feels toward others, such as attraction to the opposite sex, the same sex, both, or neither, including heterosexual, homosexual – attraction to the same sex, bisexual – attraction to more than one sex, or other orientations (e.g., asexual, pansexual). [new definition added]
Prohibitions [new sub-heading under Gender Identity and Sexual Orientation]

In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), the District shall not allow Subject Persons to provide instruction, guidance, activities, or programming regarding sexual orientation or gender identity or fluidity to students enrolled in prekindergarten through 12th grade.

A Subject Person may not use, or permit others to use, any District resource, funds, property, or personnel to adopt, support, or promote Gender Identity [replacing "Fluidity"] or sexual orientation as defined herein.

This section may not be construed to: 1. Limit a student's ability to engage in speech or expressive conduct protected by the First Amendment to the United States Constitution or by Section 8, Article I, Texas Constitution, that does not result in material disruption to school activities; 2. Limit the ability of a person who is authorized by the district to provide physical or mental health-related services to provide the services to a student, subject to any required parental consent; or 3. Prohibit an organization whose membership is restricted to one sex and whose mission does not advance a political or social agenda from meeting on a District.
For purposes of this policy, "Gender Identity [replacing "Fluidity"]" means any belief, theory or ideology that: [updated term in definition]
Social Transitioning [new section heading]

In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), social transitioning means a person's transition from the person's biological sex at birth to the opposite biological sex through the adoption of a different name, different pronouns, or other expressions of gender that deny or encourage a denial of the person's biological sex at birth.

The District shall prohibit any Subject Person from assisting a student enrolled in the district with social transitioning, including by providing any information about social transitioning or providing guidelines intended to assist a person with social transitioning.

The District shall prohibit [replacing "will not promote, require or encourage"] the use of pronoun identifiers for students, or any Subject Persons [replacing "teachers or any other persons"] in any manner that is inconsistent with the biological sex of such person as listed on: [Pronoun Usage section updated]
Prohibited Terms [new section heading]
The District shall not permit its Subject Persons to use the term "Latinx" but shall instead use the terms "Hispanic," "Hispanics," "Latino," "Latinos," "Latina" or "Latinas."

The District shall not permit its Subject Persons to use the term "minor-attracted person" and shall require the use of the term "pedophile" for any reference to sexual acts or attraction to students. Any Instructional Materials that speak to or reference pedophilia should only refer to it as an abhorrent crime.
Student First Amendment Rights [new section heading]
Nothing in this Policy may be construed to affect a student's rights under the First Amendment to the United States Constitution or Section 8, Article I, Texas Constitution.
of the social transitioning section of this [added specificity to Student or Parent Concerns section]

Additionally, if the Board determines that a district employee has assisted a student enrolled at the district with social transitioning, the board shall immediately report the violation to the Texas Commissioner of Education. [added to Student or Parent Concerns section]

Any other grievances or concerns under this Policy [replacing "A student or parent with concerns regarding instruction about controversial issues"] shall be directed to the complaint policy at DGBA (Local), FNG (Local), and GF (Local).
Discipline for Violations [new section heading]

A District employee who knowingly engages in a violation of this Board policy or assigning to another person an act prohibited by this policy shall be cause for appropriate discipline, including termination of District employees and contractors. [See DFAA, DFBA, and DFCA for procedures for discipline, including termination, as appropriate.]
Subject Persons, who are not district employees that violate this Board policy may be immediately terminated or removed by the Superintendent or designee. [See CH Local]
Policy Distribution [new section heading]
The District shall make available a copy of this policy to all District Subject Persons.
Superintendent Authority [new section heading]

The Superintendent shall develop policies and procedures to ensure compliance with this Policy. The Superintendent shall certify to the Board of Trustees that the District complies with this Policy not later than February 1, 2026.

As used in this policy, "Subject Persons" means those persons who (1) are employed by the District, (2) volunteer time or services on behalf of the District or (3) directly interact with District students. [old definition location/text removed from body of policy, moved and expanded]
Teachers shall not be compelled to discuss widely debated and currently controversial issues
a Subject Person teacher chooses to discuss a topic described above
the Subject Person teacher must explore that topic objectively
In guiding classroom discussion of controversial issues, Subject Persons teachers shall comply
Subject Person teacher shall be certain that:
If a Subject Person teacher is unsure
the Subject Person teacher may discuss
In guiding classroom discussion of controversial issues, Subject Persons teachers shall: [Classroom Discussion]
its teachers, and administrators, or any third party, [removed from Certain Instructional Requirements and Prohibitions intro]
or guilt [replaced by expanded language "discomfort, anguish, or guilt, or any other form of psychological distress"]
The District, including its Subject Persons [old phrasing before revision to remove "its teachers, and administrators, or any third party"]
Gender Fluidity Identity and Sexual Orientation [old section heading with "Fluidity" struck]

The District, including its Subject Persons, shall not teach, instruct, train or otherwise require any Subject Person or any individual or group, to adopt, support or otherwise promote Gender Fluidity (as defined herein). Additionally, Subject Persons shall not teach, instruct, train, or otherwise require any other Subject Person to teach, instruct, train, or otherwise communicate to any individual or group topics regarding sexual orientation or gender identity. [old Gender Fluidity section body removed and replaced]
For purposes of this policy, "Gender Fluidity" means [replaced with "Gender Identity"]
will not promote, require or encourage [replaced by "shall prohibit" in Pronoun Usage]
teachers or any other persons [replaced by "or any Subject Persons" in Pronoun Usage]

If the board determines that a district employee has violated this policy, the board shall immediately report the violation to the commissioner. [replaced by updated social transitioning-specific language]

timeline_local.notes: This is a comprehensive policy rewrite. Red underlined text = additions; red strikethrough = deletions. The document is largely new red-underlined text (new sections added per S.B. 12, 89th Texas Legislature). The cover page title reads 'Discuss and Possibly Adopt Revisions to Board Policy EMB (Local)' which maps to action='adopted' per the extraction rules. Many of the additions are entirely new sections with no prior counterpart. The term 'Gender Fluidity' is replaced throughout with 'Gender Identity.' The definition of 'Subject Persons' was moved from the body of the policy to a dedicated top-level defined section and slightly expanded to include 'contractors, vendors, and other individuals.'

elements.element: Expansion of 'Subject Persons' definition to include contractors, vendors, and volunteers
elements.statutory_basis: S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025) — extends prohibitions to contractors and volunteers on behalf of district
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — LegiScan enrolled text; TEA 89th Legislature guidance; ACLU fact sheet confirmed contractor/volunteer scope
elements.litigation_status: Active — GSA Network v. Morath, No. 4:25-cv-4090 (S.D. Tex.), preliminary injunction granted 2/20/2026 as to Houston ISD, Katy ISD, and Plano ISD only; Conroe ISD is NOT a named defendant; law is currently enforceable against Conroe ISD. Required by statute, though enforcement is subject to ongoing litigation as of April 7, 2026.
elements.local_additions: S.B. 12 compels extension of prohibitions to contractors and volunteers. However, the specific three-part definitional structure elevating 'Subject Persons' to a standalone defined term applied uniformly across all policy sections is a locally chosen drafting approach that goes beyond the statutory text, which does not mandate this specific definitional architecture.

elements.element: Subject Persons shall not use classroom to transmit personal political or sectarian beliefs; objectivity requirement for controversial topic discussion
elements.statutory_basis: TEC §28.0022, as enacted by S.B. 3 (87th Tex. Leg., 2nd Called Sess., eff. 12/2/2021) and further amended by S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025). NOTE: Policy cites 'HB 3979 (87th Texas Legislature)' — this is a supersession error; S.B. 3 repealed and replaced HB 3979 effective December 2, 2021, creating TEC §28.0022 as the operative current law.
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — TEA guidance letter confirming S.B. 3 superseded HB 3979 and created TEC §28.0022; ATPE analysis confirmed supersession; LegiScan S.B. 3 enrolled text
elements.litigation_status: None identified as to these specific provisions
elements.local_additions: The extension of this obligation from 'teachers' to 'Subject Persons' (including contractors and volunteers) goes beyond the statutory text of TEC §28.0022, which addresses teachers and school districts. The district chose to apply these classroom conduct standards more broadly than the statute strictly requires.

elements.element: Holidays or Commemorative Months — acknowledgment criteria limited to Presidential/Congressional/Gubernatorial proclamations and TEKS-aligned history
elements.statutory_basis: S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025) — verified as addressing holiday and commemorative month instruction restrictions
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — LegiScan enrolled bill title and subject scope; TEA 89th Legislature updates page confirms S.B. 12 guidance issued covering this provision
elements.litigation_status: Active — GSA Network v. Morath, No. 4:25-cv-4090 (S.D. Tex.); preliminary injunction of 2/20/2026 does not specifically target this provision and does not apply to Conroe ISD. Required by statute, though S.B. 12 broadly is subject to ongoing litigation as of April 7, 2026.
elements.local_additions: The specific dual-condition eligibility test closely tracks S.B. 12 language. No material local additions identified beyond restatement.

elements.element: Certain Instructional Requirements and Prohibitions — anti-CRT provisions, race/sex stereotyping ban, psychological distress prohibition, private funding ban
elements.statutory_basis: TEC §28.0022, as enacted by S.B. 3 (87th Tex. Leg., 2nd Called Sess., eff. 12/2/2021) and S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025). NOTE: Policy attribution to 'HB 3979 (87th Texas Legislature)' is a supersession error — S.B. 3 repealed HB 3979 effective December 2, 2021; S.B. 3 / TEC §28.0022 is the operative current law, further amended by S.B. 12.
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — TEA guidance letter; ATPE analysis explicitly confirming S.B. 3 superseded HB 3979; LegiScan S.B. 3 and S.B. 12 enrolled texts
elements.litigation_status: Active — GSA Network v. Morath, No. 4:25-cv-4090 (S.D. Tex.); preliminary injunction of 2/20/2026 covers DEI-adjacent provisions; does not apply to Conroe ISD. Required by statute, though enforcement is subject to ongoing litigation as of April 7, 2026.
elements.local_additions: The new prohibition 'i. Make unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments' has no verified direct statutory mandate in S.B. 12 or TEC §28.0022 and appears to be a locally adopted addition — [CITATION UNVERIFIED: no specific Texas statute or S.B. 12 provision confirmed to mandate prohibition on 'unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments' as a standalone prohibition]. The CRT/SDI definitional gloss added to the policy is a local elaboration; the statutory prohibition on teaching these concepts exists but the district added its own definitional language. The expanded psychological distress language closely tracks TEC §28.0022 as enacted by S.B. 12 and is not a local addition. Cross-references to Board Policies CH, EFA, and EFB are locally chosen.

elements.element: Student Lobbying for Course Credit prohibition
elements.statutory_basis: TEC §28.0022, as enacted by S.B. 3 (87th Tex. Leg., 2nd Called Sess., eff. 12/2/2021). NOTE: Policy attributes this to 'HB 3979 (87th Texas Legislature)' — this is a supersession error; S.B. 3 repealed and replaced HB 3979 effective December 2, 2021 and is the operative current law. S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025) further preserved and built upon this framework.
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — TEA guidance letter; ATPE analysis explicitly confirming S.B. 3 superseded HB 3979; LegiScan S.B. 3 enrolled text confirmed TEC §28.0022 creation
elements.litigation_status: None identified specific to student lobbying provision
elements.local_additions: The extension of this prohibition to 'Subject Persons' broadly (beyond 'employees' as addressed in the statutory text) may represent a local expansion of scope. Core prohibition content mirrors statutory mandate.

elements.element: Human Sexuality Instruction — written parental opt-in consent requirement and standalone consent request
elements.statutory_basis: S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025) — mandates written opt-in parental consent before human sexuality instruction; consent request must be standalone (not bundled with other materials); minimum 14-day advance notice required per statute
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — Texas AFT deep-dive analysis; LegiScan S.B. 12 enrolled text summary; S.B. 371 (89th Leg.) analysis confirming 14-day statutory minimum; TEA guidance on parental rights form
elements.litigation_status: Active — GSA Network v. Morath, No. 4:25-cv-4090 (S.D. Tex.); preliminary injunction of 2/20/2026 does not specifically target the human sexuality consent provision and does not apply to Conroe ISD. Required by statute, though S.B. 12 broadly is subject to ongoing litigation as of April 7, 2026.
elements.local_additions: Statute requires a minimum 14-day advance notice; Conroe ISD's policy specifies 30 days — this exceeds the statutory floor and is a locally discretionary addition. The detailed 10-item consent form content checklist (items 1–10, including curriculum summary, third-party identification, class period disclosure, administrator contact information, explicit checkbox language for consent/refusal, and revocation notice language) goes significantly beyond what S.B. 12 compels. Statute uses floor-setting language for notice timing; district chose to expand the consent form requirements substantially beyond what was required. These form-content elements are Locally Discretionary.

elements.element: Pornographic Material Prohibition — composite definition, possession/acquisition/instruction prohibition
elements.statutory_basis: Partial statutory basis: Tex. Penal Code §43.24 ('harmful material'); TEC §33.021 ('sexually explicit material'); Tex. Health & Safety Code §443.001 (QR code/link reference). Board of Education v. Pico, 457 U.S. 853 (1982) cited for 'pervasively vulgar or educationally unsuitable' standard.
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — statutory sections cited are real and verifiable Texas law; Pico is a confirmed SCOTUS precedent. No single statute was verified to mandate all elements of the composite definition or to compel districts to adopt this specific multi-part definitional framework.
elements.litigation_status: None identified specific to this provision
elements.local_additions: No single Texas statute compels districts to adopt this specific eight-category composite definition of 'pornographic material.' The district drew on multiple statutory definitions and court precedent and assembled them into a locally constructed omnibus definition. The prohibition on Subject Persons instructing or training others to teach pornographic material extends beyond any directly verified statutory mandate. The cross-reference to EFB(LEGAL) and EMB(Local) is locally chosen. The district exercised substantial local discretion in assembling and expanding this definition beyond any single statutory floor.

elements.element: Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion — definitions, inclusion statement prohibition, DEI duties prohibition, contracting carve-out
elements.statutory_basis: S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025) — mandates prohibition on assigning DEI duties; prohibits compelled DEI statements; prohibits trainings/programs referencing race, color, ethnicity, gender identity, or sexual orientation except as required by law; includes HBCU carve-out and historically underutilized business contracting carve-out
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — LegiScan enrolled text; TEA 89th Legislature guidance; Texas AFT deep-dive; ACLU fact sheet; multiple news analyses of S.B. 12 provisions
elements.litigation_status: Active — GSA Network v. Morath, No. 4:25-cv-4090 (S.D. Tex.); preliminary injunction of 2/20/2026 DOES block the DEI provision as applied to Houston ISD, Katy ISD, and Plano ISD. Injunction does NOT apply to Conroe ISD. Required by statute, though enforcement of the DEI provision is subject to ongoing litigation as of April 7, 2026, with injunctive relief in place in three named districts.
elements.local_additions: Item 3 of the DEI definitions section attributes the race/sex stereotyping prohibition to 'HB 3979, 87th Texas Legislature' — this is a supersession error; the operative law is TEC §28.0022 as enacted by S.B. 3 (87th Leg., 2nd Called Sess.) and amended by S.B. 12. The cross-references to Board Policies CH (Local), EFA (Local), and EFB (Local) are locally chosen. No material local additions beyond restatement and cross-referencing for core DEI prohibition elements.

elements.element: Gender Identity and Sexual Orientation — instruction prohibition for PreK–12, resource prohibition, First Amendment carve-out for students
elements.statutory_basis: S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025) — mandates prohibition on instruction, guidance, activities, or programming regarding sexual orientation or gender identity for students in PreK–12; includes First Amendment savings clause for student speech; includes carve-out for authorized health services providers
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — LegiScan enrolled text; TEA 89th Legislature guidance; Texas AFT deep-dive analysis; ACLU fact sheet confirming PreK–12 scope and savings clauses
elements.litigation_status: Active — GSA Network v. Morath, No. 4:25-cv-4090 (S.D. Tex.); preliminary injunction of 2/20/2026 covers gender identity and sexual orientation instruction provisions as applied to Houston ISD, Katy ISD, and Plano ISD. Injunction does NOT apply to Conroe ISD. Required by statute, though enforcement is subject to ongoing litigation as of April 7, 2026, with injunctive relief in place in three named districts.
elements.local_additions: The section heading change from 'Gender Fluidity' to 'Gender Identity' tracks S.B. 12 terminology. The updated definition of 'Gender Identity' replacing 'Gender Fluidity' mirrors statutory language. The carve-out for single-sex organizations not advancing a political or social agenda appears to go beyond what S.B. 12 savings clauses specifically enumerate and may be a locally added carve-out — [CITATION UNVERIFIED: no specific S.B. 12 provision confirmed to mandate this specific single-sex organization carve-out language]. The prohibition on using District resources to support or promote Gender Identity or sexual orientation closely tracks statutory language.

elements.element: Social Transitioning — definition, prohibition on Subject Person assistance, reporting requirement to Commissioner
elements.statutory_basis: S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025) — mandates prohibition on district employees assisting students with social transitioning; defines social transitioning; requires board to report violations to Texas Commissioner of Education
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — LegiScan enrolled text; TEA 89th Legislature guidance; Texas AFT deep-dive analysis confirming social transitioning prohibition and Commissioner reporting requirement
elements.litigation_status: Active — GSA Network v. Morath, No. 4:25-cv-4090 (S.D. Tex.); preliminary injunction of 2/20/2026 covers social transitioning provisions as applied to Houston ISD, Katy ISD, and Plano ISD. Injunction does NOT apply to Conroe ISD. Required by statute, though enforcement is subject to ongoing litigation as of April 7, 2026, with injunctive relief in place in three named districts.
elements.local_additions: The extension of the social transitioning prohibition to all 'Subject Persons' (including contractors and volunteers beyond district employees) may represent a local expansion of scope beyond the statutory text. The Commissioner reporting requirement mirrors S.B. 12 language and is not a local addition.

elements.element: Pronoun Usage — prohibition on use of pronouns inconsistent with biological sex
elements.statutory_basis: S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025) — prohibits use of pronouns inconsistent with biological sex at birth for students and employees
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — LegiScan enrolled text; TEA 89th Legislature guidance; Texas AFT deep-dive analysis confirming pronoun prohibition
elements.litigation_status: Active — GSA Network v. Morath, No. 4:25-cv-4090 (S.D. Tex.); preliminary injunction of 2/20/2026 covers pronoun provisions as applied to Houston ISD, Katy ISD, and Plano ISD. Injunction does NOT apply to Conroe ISD. Required by statute, though enforcement is subject to ongoing litigation as of April 7, 2026, with injunctive relief in place in three named districts.
elements.local_additions: Policy language strengthened from 'will not promote, require or encourage' to 'shall prohibit' — this tightening of mandatory language is a locally chosen drafting choice that goes beyond restating the statute but is directionally consistent with the statutory mandate. Extension to 'Subject Persons' broadly (including contractors and volunteers) may represent local expansion of scope.

elements.element: Prohibited Terms — ban on 'Latinx' and 'minor-attracted person' terminology; required use of 'Hispanic/Latino/Latina' and 'pedophile'
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — no specific Texas statute or S.B. 12 provision was verified to mandate these specific terminology prohibitions or required term substitutions
elements.local_additions: These prohibitions are entirely locally adopted. No verified Texas statute or S.B. 12 provision compels a school district to prohibit the term 'Latinx' or to mandate use of 'pedophile' rather than 'minor-attracted person.' The district exercised local discretion in adopting these terminology rules. Statute uses permissive or silent language with respect to these specific terms.

elements.element: Student First Amendment Rights savings clause
elements.statutory_basis: U.S. Const. amend. I; Tex. Const. art. I, §8 — these constitutional provisions exist independently; S.B. 12 includes a First Amendment savings clause for student speech
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — S.B. 12 confirmed to contain First Amendment savings clause for student speech; constitutional provisions are self-executing
elements.litigation_status: None identified specific to this savings clause
elements.local_additions: While S.B. 12 contains a savings clause, the district's choice to add a standalone 'Student First Amendment Rights' section heading and affirmative policy statement is a locally chosen drafting decision that goes beyond what S.B. 12 strictly requires. Statute uses permissive/savings language ('may not be construed to limit'); district chose to elevate this to a standalone policy section. Locally discretionary in its presentation, though consistent with constitutional requirements.

elements.element: Discipline for Violations — termination of employees and contractors; immediate removal of non-employee Subject Persons by Superintendent
elements.statutory_basis: S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025) — requires districts to establish consequences for violations, including disciplinary procedures for employees who violate prohibitions
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — LegiScan enrolled text; Texas AFT deep-dive analysis confirming S.B. 12 discipline requirement
elements.litigation_status: Active — GSA Network v. Morath, No. 4:25-cv-4090 (S.D. Tex.); preliminary injunction of 2/20/2026 does not specifically target discipline provisions and does not apply to Conroe ISD. Required by statute, though S.B. 12 broadly is subject to ongoing litigation as of April 7, 2026.
elements.local_additions: S.B. 12 mandates that districts establish disciplinary consequences. The specific mechanisms — cross-referencing DFAA, DFBA, and DFCA for employee procedures; granting Superintendent authority to immediately terminate or remove non-employee Subject Persons; and the reference to CH Local for contractor removal — are locally chosen implementation details that go beyond what the statute prescribes. Statute requires discipline but does not dictate these specific procedural mechanisms.

elements.element: Policy Distribution — district shall make policy available to all Subject Persons
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — no specific Texas statute or S.B. 12 provision verified to mandate this specific policy distribution requirement
elements.local_additions: This provision is entirely locally adopted. No verified statute compels this specific distribution requirement. The district chose to add this as a matter of local implementation.

elements.element: Superintendent Authority — develop compliance procedures and certify compliance to Board by February 1, 2026
elements.statutory_basis: S.B. 12 (89th Tex. Leg., eff. 9/1/2025) — requires superintendent compliance certification to the board
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — LegiScan enrolled text; TEA 89th Legislature guidance confirming superintendent certification requirement
elements.litigation_status: Active — GSA Network v. Morath, No. 4:25-cv-4090 (S.D. Tex.); does not apply to Conroe ISD. Required by statute, though S.B. 12 broadly is subject to ongoing litigation as of April 7, 2026.
elements.local_additions: S.B. 12 mandates superintendent compliance certification. The specific deadline of February 1, 2026, and the requirement that the superintendent 'develop policies and procedures to ensure compliance' are locally chosen implementation details. The statute requires certification but does not specify February 1, 2026 as the deadline — this is a locally determined compliance date.

compliance_summary: Who the Policy Covers: The updated policy now applies to a broader group called 'Subject Persons,' which includes not just employees but also volunteers and contractors working with the district. This change is required by S.B. 12 (89th Texas Legislature), which extended certain school rules to cover people beyond regular district employees.

Human Sexuality Instruction Consent: S.B. 12 requires schools to get written permission from parents before teaching human sexuality topics, and parents must receive notice at least 14 days in advance. Conroe ISD's policy sets the notice period at 30 days and adds a detailed consent form checklist, both of which go beyond what the law requires.

Gender Identity and Social Transitioning: S.B. 12 prohibits school staff from providing instruction on gender identity or sexual orientation in grades PreK–12 and from helping students socially transition to a different gender. These provisions are currently being challenged in a federal lawsuit — GSA Network v. Morath — but that case's temporary court order blocking the law does not apply to Conroe ISD.

DEI Restrictions: S.B. 12 prohibits schools from assigning diversity, equity, and inclusion duties to staff or requiring DEI-related statements and training programs. Conroe ISD's policy reflects these requirements, and while a federal court has temporarily blocked this part of S.B. 12 for three other Texas districts, that order does not currently affect Conroe ISD.

Prohibited Terminology: The policy bans the use of the term 'Latinx' and requires staff to use 'pedophile' instead of 'minor-attracted person.' No state law requires these specific terminology rules — they are choices made by the Conroe ISD board on its own.

Superintendent Compliance Deadline: S.B. 12 requires the superintendent to certify to the school board that the district is following the new rules. Conroe ISD's policy sets February 1, 2026 as the deadline for that certification, which is a specific date chosen by the district rather than one set by the law.

timeline_local.summary: This comprehensive revision to Conroe ISD's EMB (Local) policy on Teaching About Controversial Issues expands the scope of regulated persons from "teachers" to broadly defined "Subject Persons," adds multiple new sections addressing holidays/commemorative months, human sexuality instruction, pornographic material, diversity/equity/inclusion duties, social transitioning, prohibited terms, student First Amendment rights, discipline for violations, policy distribution, and superintendent authority — primarily driven by legislation from the 87th, 88th, and 89th Texas Legislative sessions including S.B. 12 and HB 3979. The revision also removes or replaces older language on gender fluidity, pronoun usage, and reporting requirements to align with updated Texas law.
changes_added: As used in this policy, "Subject Persons" means those persons who (1) are employed by the District, (2) volunteer time or services on behalf of the District (3) directly interact with District students including contractors, vendors, and other individuals. [moved/redefined at top of policy]
Subject Persons shall not use the classroom to transmit personal beliefs regarding political or sectarian issues. [replacing 'Teachers']
Subject Persons and sStudents shall ensure that, to the extent possible, discussions are conducted fairly and courteously. [replacing 'and educators']

Unless required by state or federal law or the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills, Subject Persons shall not be compelled to discuss widely debated and currently controversial issues of public policy or social affairs.
or it is required by state or federal law or the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills [added condition for discussing controversial topics]
the Subject Person must explore that topic objectively and in a manner free from political bias. [replacing 'teacher']
Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills for that grade level [added to bullet 1 under controversial topics criteria]

Holidays or Commemorative Months — In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), the District may acknowledge or teach the significance of state and federal holidays or commemorative months only if: (1) The federal or state holiday or commemorative month is established through a Presidential Proclamation, Congressional law or Resolution, Gubernatorial Proclamation, or Texas legislative law or Resolution; and (2) Fits into the themes of history and the stories of this state and the United States of America in accordance with the TEKS under Subchapter A, Chapter 28.

Except as required by state or federal law, no private funding shall be accepted by the District for the purposes of curriculum development, purchase or choice of curricular materials, teacher training, or professional development pertaining to the above topics or Board Policy CH (Local), EFA (Local) or EFB (Local).

Student Lobbying for Course Credit — In accordance with HB 3979 (87th Texas Legislature), no employee shall require, make part of a course, or award course grading or credit including extra credit for, student work for, affiliation with, or service learning in association with any organization engaged in lobbying for legislation at the local, state or federal level, or in social or public policy advocacy. No employee shall require, make part of a course, or award course grading or credit including extra credit for, political activism, lobbying, or efforts to persuade members of the legislative or executive branch to take specific actions by direct communication at the local, state or federal level, or any practicum or like activity involving social or public policy advocacy.

Instruction on Human Sexuality — No human sexuality instruction may be provided that is inconsistent with the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills. In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), before a student may be provided with human sexuality instruction by a Subject Person, the District must obtain the written consent of the student's parent. A request for written consent under this subsection: (1) May not be included with any other notification or request for written consent provided to the parent, other than the notice, and (2) Must be provided to the parent not later than the 30th day before the date on which the human sexuality instruction begins.

The consent must include: (1) A clear, plain-language summary of the human sexuality curriculum (topics covered, objectives, grade-appropriateness). (2) Identification of whether outside speakers or third-party curriculum providers will be used. (3) Dates and class periods when the instruction will occur. (4) A statement that participation requires written parental consent under S.B. 12. (5) Confirmation that the notice is provided at least 30 days prior to instruction (to comply with subsection (2)). (6) An explanation of the right to review the instructional materials before consenting. (7) A statement of the parent's right to withhold consent without penalty to the student. (8) A reminder that parents may revoke consent at any time by written notice. (9) Clear checkboxes or signature lines for: 'I give my consent for my child to participate in human sexuality instruction,' or 'I do not give my consent for my child to participate,' and Space for parent/guardian signature and date. (10) Name and contact information for the administrator or office handling the curriculum (so parents can ask questions or review materials).

Pornographic Material Prohibition — Pornographic material includes, but not limited to, 'Harmful material' as defined by Section 43.24, Penal Code; 'indecent content' that portrays sexual or excretory organs or activities in a way that is patently offensive; 'profane content' including grossly offensive language that is considered a public nuisance; a library material rated sexually explicit material by the selling library material vendor; 'sexually explicit material' as defined by Education Code 33.021; library material containing indecent content or profane content; any material that is pervasively vulgar or educationally unsuitable as referenced in Board of Education v. Pico; any material that refers a person to an Internet website containing pornographic material as described in this section of this Policy, including by use of a link or QR code, as defined by Section 443.001 of the Texas Health and Safety Code, or any other material legally prohibited from inclusion in a public school library. [See EFB(LEGAL) and EMB(Local)]. The possession, acquisition, and purchase of pornographic library materials is prohibited.

Unless required by state or federal law or the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills, instructional materials and library materials shall not include pornographic materials. Subject persons shall not instruct, or train any Subject Person to teach, support, or promote Pornographic Material.

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion — In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), diversity, equity, and inclusion duties means: (1) Influencing hiring or employment practices with respect to race, sex, color, or ethnicity except as necessary to comply with state or federal antidiscrimination laws; (2) Promoting differential treatment of or providing special benefits to individuals on the basis of race, color, or ethnicity; (3) Required any District employee or student to engage in training, orientation, or therapy that presents any form of race or sex stereotyping or blame on the basis of race or sex. (HB 3979, 87th Texas Legislature). (4) Developing or implementing policies, procedures, trainings, activities, or programs that reference race, color, ethnicity, gender identity, or sexual orientation except: (A) for the purpose of student recruitment efforts by colleges and universities designated as historically black colleges and universities in collaboration with school districts or open-enrollment charter schools; or (B) as necessary to comply with state or federal law. (5) Developing or producing Equity Audits based on race, color, ethnicity, gender identity, or sexual orientation, except the District may analyze school-based cause and take steps to eliminate unlawful discriminatory practices as necessary to address achievement gaps and differentials described by Texas Education Code Section 39.053. (6) Establishing offices or employment positions or assignments related to diversity, equity, and inclusion duties.

Inclusion Statement — In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), the District may not compel, require, induce, or solicit any person to provide a diversity, equity, and inclusion statement or giving preferential consideration to any person based on the provision of a diversity, equity, and inclusion statement.

Prohibition — In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), except as required by state or federal law, a school district: (1) may not assign diversity, equity, and inclusion duties to any person; and (2) shall prohibit a district employee, contractor, or volunteer from engaging in diversity, equity, and inclusion duties at, for, or on behalf of the district.

Contracting Clarification — Nothing in this Policy may be construed to limit or prohibit the District from contracting with historically underutilized businesses or businesses owned by members of a minority group or by women in accordance with applicable state law.

Critical Race Theory and Systemic Discrimination Ideologies are generally defined as academic frameworks that laws, policies, and institutions create and maintain racial inequality, even when they appear neutral. [added to CRT/SDI section]
discomfort, anguish, or any other form of psychological distress [added to item 1(e) under prohibited instructional content, replacing 'or guilt']
Make unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments. [added as item 1(i) under prohibited instructional content]
provide guidance or activities, discuss, require an understanding, [added to item 1 prohibited actions list]
Gender Identity [replacing 'Fluidity' in section heading and definitions]

Sexual orientation means sexual attraction a person feels toward others, such as attraction to the opposite sex, the same sex, both, or neither, including heterosexual, homosexual – attraction to the same sex, bisexual – attraction to more than one sex, or other orientations (e.g., asexual, pansexual). [new definition added]

Prohibitions — In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), the District shall not allow Subject Persons to provide instruction, guidance, activities, or programming regarding sexual orientation or gender identity or fluidity to students enrolled in prekindergarten through 12th grade.

A Subject Person may not use, or permit others to use, any District resource, funds, property, or personnel to adopt, support, or promote Gender Identity Fluidity or sexual orientation as defined herein.

This section may not be construed to: (1) Limit a student's ability to engage in speech or expressive conduct protected by the First Amendment to the United States Constitution or by Section 8, Article I, Texas Constitution, that does not result in material disruption to school activities; (2) Limit the ability of a person who is authorized by the district to provide physical or mental health-related services to provide the services to a student, subject to any required parental consent; or (3) Prohibit an organization whose membership is restricted to one sex and whose mission does not advance a political or social agenda from meeting on a District.

Social Transitioning — In accordance with S.B. 12, (89th Texas Legislature), social transitioning means a person's transition from the person's biological sex at birth to the opposite biological sex through the adoption of a different name, different pronouns, or other expressions of gender that deny or encourage a denial of the person's biological sex at birth.

The District shall prohibit any Subject Person from assisting a student enrolled in the district with social transitioning, including by providing any information about social transitioning or providing guidelines intended to assist a person with social transitioning.
The District shall prohibit the use of pronoun identifiers for students, or any Subject Persons [replacing 'will not promote, require or encourage' and 'teachers or any other persons']
Prohibited Terms — The District shall not permit its Subject Persons to use the term 'Latinx' but shall instead use the terms 'Hispanic,' 'Hispanics,' 'Latino,' 'Latinos,' 'Latina' or 'Latinas.'

The District shall not permit its Subject Persons to use the term 'minor-attracted person' and shall require the use of the term 'pedophile' for any reference to sexual acts or attraction to students. Any Instructional Materials that speak to or reference pedophilia should only refer to it as an abhorrent crime.

Student First Amendment Rights — Nothing in this Policy may be construed to affect a student's rights under the First Amendment to the United States Constitution or Section 8, Article I, Texas Constitution.
of the social transitioning section of [added to Student or Parent Concerns reporting trigger]

Additionally, if the Board determines that a district employee has assisted a student enrolled at the district with social transitioning, the board shall immediately report the violation to the Texas Commissioner of Education.
Any other grievances or concerns under this Policy shall be directed to the complaint policy at DGBA (Local), FNG (Local), and GF (Local). [replacing prior language]

Discipline for Violations — A District employee who knowingly engages in a violation of this Board policy or assigning to another person an act prohibited by this policy shall be cause for appropriate discipline, including termination of District employees and contractors. [See DFAA, DFBA, and DFCA for procedures for discipline, including termination, as appropriate.]
Subject Persons, who are not district employees that violate this Board policy may be immediately terminated or removed by the Superintendent or designee. [See CH Local]
Policy Distribution — The District shall make available a copy of this policy to all District Subject Persons.

Superintendent Authority — The Superintendent shall develop policies and procedures to ensure compliance with this Policy. The Superintendent shall certify to the Board of Trustees that the District complies with this Policy not later than February 1, 2026.

Additionally, Subject Persons shall not teach, instruct, train, or otherwise require any other Subject Person to teach, instruct, train, or otherwise communicate to any individual or group topics regarding sexual orientation or gender identity. timeline_local.notes: This is a substantially rewritten policy (first reading). The redline is extensive — most of the document is new red-underlined text indicating new sections added in their entirety. Key structural changes include: broadening regulated persons from 'teachers' to 'Subject Persons'; adding entirely new sections on Holidays/Commemorative Months, Human Sexuality Instruction with detailed consent requirements, Pornographic Material Prohibition, DEI definitions and prohibitions, Social Transitioning, Prohibited Terms, Student First Amendment Rights, Discipline for Violations, Policy Distribution, and Superintendent Authority. Some color ambiguity existed in a few passages but context was clear. The 'Fluidity' to 'Identity' rename in the Gender section heading was captured. The Social and Emotional Learning section appears unchanged (black text) and was not included.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's EMB Local Policy significantly expands the district's controversial issues instructional policy by adding new sections based on 87th, 88th, and 89th Texas legislative sessions. Key additions include a definition of "Subject Persons," prohibitions on teaching Critical Race Theory and related ideologies, restrictions on divisive Social and Emotional Learning (SEL) concepts, and new sections prohibiting instruction or promotion of Gender Fluidity and non-biological pronoun usage.
timeline_local.changes_added: ["As used in this policy, "Subject Persons" means those persons who (1) are employed by the District, (2) volunteer time or services on behalf of the District or and (3) directly interact with District students.", "Teachers shall not be compelled to discuss widely debated and currently controversial issues of public policy or social affairs. However, in the event a teacher chooses to discuss a topic described above, the teacher must explore that topic objectively and in a manner free from political bias. In guiding classroom discussion of controversial issues, teachers shall comply with the instructional requirements and prohibitions imposed under Texas law and by the District.", "5. Avoid any attempt to coerce or persuade students to adopt the teacher's point of view.", "5. 6. Comply with the instructional requirements and prohibitions imposed under Texas law.", "Certain Instructional Requirements and Prohibitions [section heading]", "The District, including its teachers and administrators, shall not:", "1. Require, teach, make part of a course or training, or otherwise instruct any Subject Persons or students that: a. One race or sex is inherently superior to another race or sex; b. An individual, by virtue of the individual's race or sex, is inherently racist, sexist, or oppressive, whether consciously or unconsciously; c. An individual should be discriminated against or receive adverse treatment solely or partly because of the individual's race or sex; d. An individual's moral character, standing, or worth is partially or necessarily determined by the individual's race or sex; e. An individual, by virtue of the individual's race or sex, bears responsibility, blame, or guilt for actions committed by other members of the same race or sex; f. Meritocracy or traits such as a hard work ethic are racist or sexist or were created by members of a particular race or group to oppress members of another race or group; g. The delivery of slaves and the advent of slavery in the territory that is now the United States constituted the true founding of the United States; or h. With respect to their relationship to American values, slavery and racism are anything other than deviations from, betrayals of, or failures to live up to the authentic founding principles of the United States, which include liberty and equality;", "2. Teach, instruct, train, introduce, discuss or require an understanding of The 1619 Project, which began as a compilation of essays, poems, and other works originally published in The New York Times Magazine in August 2019 but has become the impetus for the development of resources, programs, and curricula for educators; or", "3. Teach, instruct, advocate, promote, or discuss any ideas, beliefs, concepts, theories, principles, rules, thoughts, or impressions that have any connection to, relationship with, refer to, are influenced by, or are otherwise consistent with so-called "Critical Race Theory" or systemic discrimination ideologies, including, but not limited to, those ideologies set forth in this section of this policy, as such ideologies have been understood, developed, taught, interpreted, defined/redefined by individuals, organizations, and others who have created, defined, redefined, interpreted, taught, instructed, advocated, promoted, or discussed or used to understand so-called "Critical Race Theory" or systemic discrimination ideologies. This subsection, along with subsection (1) and (2) above, shall be collectively referred to as "Critical Race Theory or Systemic Discrimination Ideologies" or "CRT/SDI"'.", "Social and Emotional Learning Concepts [section heading]", "Most traditional social and emotional learning (SEL) teachings are consistent with the District's general education goals, particularly concepts relating to the development of self-awareness, individualism, self-reliance, self-motivation, communication, conflict resolution, and interpersonal skills that are vital for academic, professional, and life success.", "However, some SEL concepts can be potentially divisive, conflict with District policy, and be inconsistent with the District's education goals. As such, the District, including its Subject Persons, shall not teach, instruct, train or otherwise require any Subject Person or any individual or group, to adopt, support, or otherwise promote SEL concepts that are divisive, conflict with District policy, or are inconsistent with the District's education goals.", "For the avoidance of doubt, the District will continue to support and promote the following ideologies and concepts, which are generally consistent with the positive components of SEL and with the general educational goals of the state and District: individualism; a rejection of victimhood mentality; conflict resolution techniques; aspiration to serve as entrepreneurs and spiritual and community leaders; financial self-sufficiency; importance of the nuclear family; liberty, hard work and perseverance as the basis for a successful society; and the virtues of self-discipline, forgiveness, patience, kindness, determination, hope, thankfulness, reliability, honesty, industry and responsibility.", "Gender Fluidity [section heading]", "The District, including its Subject Persons, shall not teach, instruct, train or otherwise require any Subject Person or any individual or group, to adopt, support or otherwise promote Gender Fluidity (as defined herein). Additionally, Subject Persons shall not teach, instruct, train, or otherwise require any other Subject Person to teach, instruct, train, or otherwise communicate to any individual or group topics regarding sexual orientation or gender identity.", "For purposes of this policy, "Gender Fluidity" means any belief, theory or ideology that: 1. Espouses the view that gender is merely a social construct; 2. Espouses the view that it is possible for a person to be any gender or none (i.e., non-binary); 3. Espouses the view that an individual's biological sex should attempt to be changed to "match" a gender different from that person's biological sex; and 4. Supports hormone therapy or other medical treatments or procedures to temporarily or permanently alter a person's body so that it "matches" a gender different from that person's biological sex.", "A Subject Person may not use, or permit others to use, any District resource, funds, property, or personnel to adopt, support, or promote Gender Fluidity as defined herein.", "Pronoun Usage [section heading]", "The District will not promote, require or encourage the use of pronoun identifiers for students, teachers or any other persons in any manner that is inconsistent with the biological sex of such person as listed on: 1. The student's official birth certificate; or 2. If the student's official birth certificate is unobtainable, another government-issued record.", "However, to the extent a student (with the written consent of such student's parent or legal guardian), parent or legal guardian has specifically, in writing, requested or directed the use of a specific pronoun for that particular student, a teacher or administrator interacting with the student may comply with such request.", "A Subject Person shall not be permitted to require or request a student to use a pronoun to refer to such Subject Person in a manner that is inconsistent with the biological sex of such Subject Person as listed on: 1. The Subject Person's official birth certificate; or 2. If the Subject Person's official birth certificate is unobtainable, another government-issued record.", "A parent of a student enrolled in the district or a district employee may report to the board of trustees a suspected violation of this gender fluidity and/or pronoun usage policy. The board shall investigate any suspected violation and determine whether the violation occurred. If the board determines that a district employee has violated this policy, the board shall immediately report the violation to the commissioner."]
changes_removed: A teacher selecting topics for discussion in the classroom shall be adequately informed about the issue and capable of providing instruction on the subject, free from personal bias.
timeline_local.notes: The cover page title reads "Consider and Adopt Changes to Local Policies: EMB Miscellaneous Instructional Policies: Teaching About Controversial Issues," which maps to the 'adopted' action. Red underlined text is clearly distinguishable throughout. The word "or" replacing "and" in the Subject Persons definition (page 2) also appears to be a strikethrough/addition inline edit. The blue-highlighted text on page 5 (violation reporting paragraph) appears to be new added language as well, styled slightly differently but contextually new. All major additions are from HB 3979 (87th session), SB 123 (87th session), and SB 12 (89th session) as noted in the margin comments.

description_local: This policy sets guidelines for how controversial or sensitive topics must be handled during classroom instruction. It applies to a broader group of school district employees — not just classroom teachers, but potentially other staff involved in instruction or educational activities. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it shapes how schools approach discussions of complex or contested subjects, helping ensure that multiple perspectives are considered and that students are not unduly influenced by the personal views of school staff.
